title: Gaming Accessibility Fundamentals
creator: Microsoft
description: A free learning path introducing disability, common barriers in games, assistive technology, accessibility principles, and the roles different team members play in inclusive development.
useful: It establishes shared foundations before a team jumps into long checklists or isolated feature decisions.
audience: Anyone entering game development, including non-specialists who need a common accessibility vocabulary.
learning: Describe disability through barriers rather than stereotypes
Recognize common obstacles across game systems
Identify how each development role can improve access
